response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with a long track record, but the profile does not match this embedded security/networking role. Two hard requirements are unmet: native-level Japanese is absent (only English, Romanian, and basic German are listed), and strong C-for-UNIX proficiency is not demonstrated—C/C++ appears only briefly (National Instruments, ~6 months, and a bachelor thesis), with the bulk of the career in JavaScript/TypeScript, D, and web platforms. There is no evidence of LAN-switch firmware, Linux kernel source modification, L2/L3 networking, or security technologies such as MACsec, 802.1X, or MAC authentication. Despite strong general engineering credentials, the gap against the core requirements is too large.
strengths: Extensive, varied software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded systems
Some low-level C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI) and Linux familiarity
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, and demonstrated knowledge sharing
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency (a stated hard requirement)
Limited C-for-UNIX depth; career centers on JS/TypeScript and D, not embedded firmware
No evidence of switch/networking, MACsec/802.1X security, or Linux kernel source modification
